text.title: Error Wrapping and Sentinel Errors
text.summary: fmt.Errorf with %w for wrapping, package-level sentinel vars, and repository-boundary translation of storage errors.

rule.statement: Wrap errors exactly once per layer using fmt.Errorf("verb context %s: %w", arg, err). The verb describes what THIS layer was doing. Never wrap with %v — it drops the errors.Is chain.
rule.rationale: %v formats the error message but breaks errors.Is and errors.As traversal. %w wraps the error and preserves the chain. Wrapping more than once per layer produces noisy redundant context in logs.

antipattern.description: fmt.Errorf("getting user: %v", err) — using %v instead of %w.
antipattern.reason: errors.Is(wrapped, ErrNotFound) returns false. The entire sentinel-error machinery stops working.

rule.statement: Define all sentinel errors once in a single apperror package. Never call errors.New("not found") at individual call sites — each call allocates a new pointer and breaks errors.Is matching.
rule.rationale: errors.Is uses pointer equality for errors created by errors.New. Two different calls to errors.New("not found") produce two non-equal errors. Centralise in package vars.

rule.statement: Translate storage-layer sentinels (sql.ErrNoRows, pgx.ErrNoRows, mongo.ErrNoDocuments) into domain sentinels (ErrNotFound) at the repository layer. Never let storage errors escape to the service or handler.
rule.rationale: Service code should not know which storage engine the repository uses. Leaking sql.ErrNoRows to a service couples it to the database driver.
